event_blocks.summary: The Beta Squad challenge commences with teams racing to get furthest from London within 10 hours and a budget of £69 per person, facing three challenges: stroking an animal, convincing a stranger an object is a landmark, and using five different transport modes. Team 1, comprising Chunkz and AJ, begins by cycling electric bikes towards Euston Station, with AJ navigating and Chunkz expressing surprise at his own speed and near-crashes, aiming to catch a train to Manchester or Liverpool. They initially faced a budget issue with train tickets costing £213 against their £207 limit, prompting a plan to earn money. Meanwhile, Team 2, including Nico, Sharky, and Kenny, engages in a humorous 'money laundering scheme' at Piccadilly Circus, where the Host, dressed in winter wear, pretends to teach spells to individuals dressed as wizards and a woman in a Gryffindor scarf for money, with their budget shown as £208. The Host initiates the challenge by instructing Team 1 to head north by train, with AJ checking his phone for ticket prices and Chunkz joining the discussion as they decide to use bikes to Euston before boarding a train, needing to earn money within an hour and 20 minutes before departure. AJ confidently explains their plan to reach Euston Station and catch a train, impressing Chunkz with his quick thinking. The narrative highlights their urgent mission to make money amidst busy London streets, with the Host reminding them of the ticking clock. Chunkz exclaims his speed on an electric bike, nearly crashing, while AJ rides alongside, confirming their first mode of transport and their destination, Euston Station. AJ estimates their remaining time to reach the station, urging his teammate to hurry. A narrator notes Team 1's journey while Team 2 begins their 'money laundering schemes,' which involves the Host playfully extorting a family for £1 by offering to teach them spells, a move sarcastically described by the narrator as a low moral compass.

Shroom.description: A man in a dark jacket and beanie, seen riding a bicycle and dancing with Sharky, part of Team 2.

Host.description: A man in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, holding a phone, who engages in playful challenges, asking "You guys have your passports?" and offering to teach "spells for some money," then negotiating the price to "One pound is fine," and playfully prompting a woman to perform a magic trick.

Sharky.description: A man in a black jacket and cap, seen riding a bicycle and later dancing, identified by the voiceover as part of Team 2.

Woman in Gryffindor Scarf.description: A woman wearing a black coat, maroon Gryffindor scarf, and holding a wand, who performs a magic trick for the Host in Piccadilly Circus, whispering the spell "Shishmania... Shishmania... It makes them disappear."

raw_segments: The video opens with a dramatic aerial shot of a cityscape under a stormy sky, overlaid with a glowing green map of the UK and the text 'TODAY ON BETA SQUAD'. A deep male voiceover announces, "Today on Beta Squad, they are racing to see who can get the furthest away from London." The scene transitions to a bright, stylized graphic of the UK map with 'LONDON' marked, as the voiceover continues, explaining the teams are "split into two teams." Team 1, featuring Chunkz, a man in a patterned sweater and black beanie, and AJ, a man in a dark hoodie and cap, is introduced visually in a lively urban setting with bold text overlays. During their introduction, Chunkz emits an "Ow!" sound, followed by AJ exclaiming, "Oh, what's going on?" The voiceover then introduces "Team 2, which has Nico, Sharky, and Kenny," while visually Sharky, a man in a black jacket and cap, and Shroom, a man in a dark jacket and beanie, are seen riding bicycles and interacting energetically on a city street. A lively "Gather 'round, gather 'round!" is heard from one of the team members. The teams are then shown receiving instructions from a host, a man in a dark jacket with a fur-lined hood, as the voiceover states they will be given "a range of different challenges for them to do and must complete before the time runs out." A countdown timer appears against a sunset backdrop, showing '09:59:57', with the text 'THEY HAVE EXACTLY 10 HOURS', as the voiceover confirms they have "exactly 10 hours to see how far they can get." The challenges are then revealed in a series of blue-tinted, glitch-effect title cards: Challenge 1 requires stroking an animal with video proof; Challenge 2 involves convincing a member of the public that something is a famous landmark and taking a picture with them; and Challenge 3 mandates using five different modes of transport, after which they can use any vehicle, all while adhering to a budget of "69 pounds per person." The segment ends with a transition to Chunkz, signaling the start of the action, as the voiceover begins to say, "Now, let's go to Chunkz to..."

The video opens dynamically at Piccadilly Circus in London, England, with an energetic background track and the sounds of a bustling city. The Host, dressed in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and a black beanie, greets the teams with a high-five, loudly initiating the challenge and declaring, "the challenge starts now!" He instructs them to go north by train, aiming for Manchester or Liverpool. AJ, wearing a dark hoodie and a black cap with a Jumpman logo, checks his phone, initially stating a train ticket costs "£71," though the visual context indicates the actual total cost is £213, which exceeds their £207 budget. Chunkz, in a colorful patterned sweater and black beanie, joins the discussion. The team quickly decides to start with a bike to Euston and then take a train, but first, they need to earn money. The Host confirms they have "an hour and 20" minutes until the train departs. As AJ and Chunkz walk through the busy London streets, passing traffic lights, pedestrians, and iconic architecture like a red phone booth, AJ confidently explains their plan to reach Euston Station and catch a train to Manchester. Chunkz, impressed, questions how AJ devised the plan so quickly, to which AJ confidently replies, "Because I'm ready, I'm on point today." The urgent city sounds and background music underscore their focused mission to make money before their departure, with the Host reminding them, "Time's already going."

The video opens with a fast-paced, blurred street view in London, transitioning to Chunkz, in a colorful patterned sweater and black beanie, exclaiming "Look how fast I am!" while riding an electric bike. He then reacts with a surprised "What the f-" after a near crash. AJ, in a dark hoodie and black Jumpman cap, rides alongside him, checking his phone. AJ states, "Okay, so we're on our first mode of transport, we're on bikes," and asks Chunkz, "When's the last time you were on a bike?" Chunkz replies, "Bro, years, it's been years," and adds, "I almost saw you crash when you went there." AJ acknowledges, "Yeah, I know, I know," and then comments, "You can't beep at him." On-screen text confirms their mode of transport as 'BIKE 1/5' and highlights their goal: 'WE'RE GOING TO EUSTON STATION'. AJ continues, detailing their plan: "We're gonna take the bike straight to Euston and we have our little phones in there now. I think 13 minutes, maybe 25 'cause it's me." He then urges someone, "Jump in, we're about to go, you're making us late, come on!" before declaring, "We're at the station!" The scene shifts to an aerial map displaying a green route from Piccadilly Circus to Euston Station, with the team's faces superimposed on the path, as a narrator's voiceover states, "As Team 1 begins their journey, the other team began their money laundering schemes." The narrative then cuts to a street scene in Piccadilly Circus where the Host, in a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, holding a phone, confronts a group of three men dressed in black winter wear, including one in a bucket hat, and AJ. The Host playfully asks, "You guys have your passports?" and then offers, "I can teach you some spells for some money." One of the men from the group responds, "Um, so we're wizards, we go to the School of Harry Potter." The Host questions, "Are you sure?" to which the man replies, "Yeah, so we can teach spells for two pounds." The Host negotiates, "One pound is fine," and after receiving it, says, "Thank you, thank you." The Host then turns to a woman in a Gryffindor scarf and cloak, holding a wand, and playfully says, "Okay, I'll teach you a spell," prompting her to perform a magic trick with a smile, whispering the spell "Shishmania... Shishmania... It makes them disappear." On-screen text humorously labels the interaction as 'THEIR MONEY LAUNDERING SCHEME' and 'EXTORTING AN INNOCENT FAMILY', while the team's budget is displayed as £208. A narrator's voiceover sarcastically comments, "It seems that this team's moral compass is at an all-time low, as they extort this innocent family for one pound." The atmosphere is lighthearted and comedic, blending urban exploration with playful street interactions.

event_blocks.summary: In a festive Piccadilly Circus adorned with Christmas decorations and a 'WELCOME TO CHRISTMAS' banner, the Host, wearing a black jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, playfully interacts with a Woman in Gryffindor Scarf, who holds a wand in front of the Hard Rock Cafe; as she performs a magic trick, the Host exclaims 'THAT'S GOOD.' A narrator interjects, stating, 'And then they happily break the law as they begin to put on a show without a legal permit,' while the Host's voice explains, 'I think the best thing we can do right now, in a place like Leicester Square, is perform and hope that people give us money.' The scene shifts to the Host, Sharky in a black jacket and cap, and Shroom in a dark jacket and beanie, dancing energetically in the bustling open plaza, with the Host acting as an MC, announcing, 'Ladies and gentlemen, this is the first boxing dancing show! Gather 'round, gather 'round! These guys can do backflips! I'll tell you that now.' As on-screen text reinforces their performance with phrases like 'THE LAW AS THEY BEGIN' and 'THESE GUYS CAN DO BACKFLIPS,' they execute synchronized dance moves, including arm gestures and backflips, moving past storefronts like Greggs and McDonald’s, with the Piccadilly Circus clock tower in view. The Host continues to engage the crowd, urging, 'It's rude to just walk past while people are performing,' and asking, 'Do you want to watch the boxing dancing show?' with a laugh, before hyping the performance with 'Let's go for that knockout blow! Three, two, one! Woo!' and concluding with 'And that's crazy dancing at the end!' The group finishes with a high-energy dance sequence and enthusiastic clapping, prompting the Host to shout, 'Yeah! Oh, clapping, clapping, yeah! We got clapping, yeah! Yeah! We got clapping, yeah!' as 'OH CLAPPING COMING YEAH' appears on screen. Amidst the lively atmosphere, a male voice asks, 'So where's the money now?' followed by laughter. A Young Woman in a Red Hoodie approaches, takes a selfie, and asks, 'Do you have a hat for the show?' to which the Host responds with a thumbs-up, before the group continues their performance near a 'HOME OF LA CLIQUE' sign.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a woman in a black coat and Gryffindor scarf performing a magic trick with a wand outside the Hard Rock Cafe, eliciting surprise from the Host, who inquires about a tip, to which she replies she only has card. The scene transitions to a busy city street where Chunkz, wearing a colorful sweater and beanie, rides an electric bike, exclaiming his fingers are "mashed up" and urging his companions to "Let's go, guys!". AJ, in a dark hoodie and Jumpman cap, follows on his own electric bike, checking his phone and noting that Chunkz "can literally go through" with his bike. Upbeat music accompanies their ride past cars and pedestrians, with Chunkz showing excitement and AJ focused on their destination. They arrive at Euston Station, identified by signage, where AJ discusses train tickets to Manchester, stating they have £138 from £69 each, but Chunkz notes the expense of weekend tickets. AJ then reveals the tickets are £142, leaving them £4 short, prompting Chunkz to ask, "Who has £4?". Later, AJ, near Euston Station with on-screen graphics showing a budget of £143 and ticket cost of £142, asks a man in a blue jacket for five pounds, gratefully accepting it and declaring, "All right, cool, let's book these tickets and go to Manchester." The scene shifts to a city plaza where Sharky, Shroom, and the Host, all in black winter jackets and beanies, encounter a young woman in a red hoodie. The Host explains their situation: "We're trying to get there today... we don't currently have the funds. If we can do a Scouse accent that impresses you, you give us a tiny bit of cash." They then meet the woman in the black coat and Gryffindor scarf; the Host asks her for a tenner, which she provides, performing a magic trick that astonishes him, Sharky, and the Host, who exclaims, "Oh my gosh! No way! No!" and "We got a tenner!". After thanking her profusely, the group walks past Christmas market signage and the Hard Rock Cafe. The Host calculates their updated budget to £218, expressing relief and joy, and suggests they "go towards the bikes now" to get to Euston. The segment concludes with them walking past docked electric bikes, with a red double-decker bus in the background, as a narrator states, "Upon finding the bikes, the boys rushed to hire them out, and the chaos ensues." The narrative then details a sunlit London street where AJ and the Host, Nico, stand near blue electric bikes. Nico confidently estimates the ride to Euston at 10 minutes and urges, "Let's go, let's go, let's go, let's go!" mounting a 'Lime' bike, with AJ following. Their ride through city streets becomes chaotic, with Nico exclaiming, "Oh my gosh, oh my gosh, careful, careful, careful. Oh, almost died immediately," after a near collision with a van. Amidst tense music, he shouts about almost dying, his confidence wavering, "I am a strong bike rider. I'm telling you now. Oh, guys! This is a mess! Oh my God, I'm about to fall in! I'm not confident! This is a bad idea! Ooooooh! Where's Sharky? I'm not enjoying this! I'm behind! This is the worst thing I've ever done! Oh, I almost jumped into a van! Feel like I'm gonna die. Feel like I'm gonna die." After a shaky pause at a crosswalk, Nico, calmer, attempts to restart, saying, "Okay, let's try again with the GoPro. I've made it! Ah! Sorry. Almost died. Oh, are we done? Are we done? Euston's there. Oh, wow! Oh, we got here quick! Okay. What's the damage?" He dismounts to join AJ and Sharky in a plaza with classical architecture, checking phones and discussing the journey's cost and time, with on-screen text showing 'MODE OF TRANSPORT BIKE 1/5' and the 'TEAM'S BUDGET' decreasing to £210. Sharky gestures as Nico looks concerned, and a male narrator reveals, "It turns out that Nico took 25 minutes when it should have been 13, meaning that the boys had..." highlighting the travel time discrepancy.

event_blocks.summary: AJ, Sharky, and the Host, clad in winter attire, initially discussed their plan to travel to Manchester from a sunlit city plaza, with the narrator highlighting their urgent need for £3 due to a previous £8 loss. Sharky confirmed they had only two minutes before their train departed, prompting AJ's urgent call to action. The group then engaged in a fast-paced sequence, running through city streets towards Euston Station, with the Host and AJ riding electric bikes to an upbeat electronic music track. Inside the crowded Euston Station, they navigated towards the departure boards, seeking the 12:08 Manchester Piccadilly train. Chunkz joined them, humorously speculating about others being on the same train, while AJ and Chunkz discussed their budget. The Host breathlessly confirmed their platform location and journey en route to Manchester Piccadilly, admitting they were 'very far.' As urgency mounted, they were seen running towards the exit, the Host exclaiming, 'Run, guys, run, run, run!' and admitting he couldn't run any faster. The segment concluded outside the station with the Host desperately asking for £3 amidst an overcast setting. Later, the Host, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom celebrated successfully making their train outside 'The Podium,' with the Host exclaiming 'MANNY HERE WE COME.' They entered Euston Station, gathering around a ticket machine to check their budget, which AJ detailed as £218.10, revealing they had an extra £2.79 but needed to make 49p after tax. Shroom confirmed he had 50p, leading to celebrations and the Host acknowledging their potential win. They rushed towards platform six, with AJ noting their second mode of transport was complete and the Host urging them to board before the train departed in eight minutes, despite AJ's comment about potentially missing it and getting their cardio in.

A narrator's voice explains their previous loss of £8, highlighting their urgent need for £3. AJ's urgent voice asks, "How long have we got left till the train goes?", to which Sharky calmly replies, "Literally like two minutes." AJ then exclaims, "Okay, we need to go then. Let's go, let's go!" The group transitions to a fast-paced sequence, running through city streets towards Euston Station, with the Host and AJ now riding electric bikes, all set to an upbeat, driving electronic music track. Inside the bustling Euston Station, the group navigates the crowded platform, checking digital departure boards for the 12:08 Manchester Piccadilly train. Chunkz, now wearing a white cap and black jacket, joins them, his playful voice speculating, "Imagine their plan is to go on the same train as us," which AJ finds "hilarious," while Sharky deems it "bad." Chunkz confidently asserts, "There's no way they beat us. We're already going Manny," as he and AJ converse about their budget. The Host's energetic and slightly breathless voice describes their journey, "We're currently at the platform and we are en route to Manchester Piccadilly... we are very far." As urgency builds, they are seen running towards the exit, the Host's voice exclaiming, "Run, guys, run, run, run!" and then breathlessly admitting, "I genuinely can't run any faster than this right now." The final frames show them outside the station, still in motion, with the Host desperately asking, "Anyone got £3? £3? £3?", indicating a last-minute need for cash amidst the overcast outdoor setting.

The video segment opens with the group—Host, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom—standing outside a modern building labeled 'The Podium,' visibly excited and celebrating their successful task. The Host, in his black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, exclaims 'OH MY GOD WAIT' as they realize they’ve made their train, with AJ adding a quick 'Brother.' Amidst celebratory shouts and the Host's exclamations of 'Oh my god,' 'Thank you, man,' and 'Oh, amazing,' the group rushes through a city plaza, passing a 'TAXIS' sign and a 'DISCOVER' banner. The Host raises his arms in triumph, shouting 'MANNY HERE WE COME,' and declares, 'Guys, we are making our train!' with further celebratory cheers and shouts of 'Let's go!' and 'Woo!' as they enter Euston Station. They gather around a blue self-service ticket machine, checking their budget displayed on-screen as £215. AJ, in his dark hoodie and Jumpman cap, explains, 'More on there than it is on here, so we're gonna have to switch. £230 here, £218.10 there,' as he holds up his phone to show the team’s updated budget of £218.10, revealing they have an extra £2.79. He clarifies, 'We're in a bit of a situation. There's £2.49 tax, so we've only got an extra £2. So we now have to make 49p.' Sharky, in his black jacket and cap, reacts with surprise, while Shroom, in his dark jacket and beanie, gives a thumbs-up and confirms 'Yeah' when asked if he has 50p. The group celebrates their financial gain, with the Host exclaiming 'That is... Oh, that's even better,' and 'Because of you, we might win this challenge.' The Host then shouts, 'There we go, you're going to Manchester Piccadilly!' and 'Yes!' as they rush toward the platform, passing the 'Euston Station' signage and a large departure board. AJ adds, 'That's our second mode of transport done.' The Host urges, 'We got, we got eight minutes to catch our train. All right, let's go, let's go. Boarding, it's boarding, platform six!' Someone, likely AJ, adds, 'We actually might miss this,' and 'Get my cardio in for the day,' as the group moves quickly through the bustling station, their excitement palpable.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins on a bustling train platform as the Host, clad in a black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood and beanie, leads his group, including Chunkz, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom, onto a moving train. Inside, amidst laughter and animated conversation, they react with surprise to the destination display showing 'Stoke-On-Trent' and 'Stockport.' A playful rivalry emerges as they discover another group on the same train, leading to exchanges about their destinations, with Chunkz displaying 'CHUNKZ IS THERE' on his phone. Niko, introduced as 'very immature,' initiates a 'childish battle' with a young boy in a purple and pink shirt, who is encouraged by the group to take pictures. The boy humorously states, 'I didn't get a picture,' but is praised as a 'legend.' Later, AJ playfully teases the boy about a 'fiver' and a 'test for loyalty,' proposing to 'split the profits' from a future game, to which the boy negotiates, 'you've gotta up your price sorry.' Chunkz waves at a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walking down the aisle. AJ continues the playful banter with Niko, accusing him of stealing money and instructing him to repeat phrases like 'Niko stole money off us,' culminating in mock applause for Niko's 'incredible' actions. The scene shifts to Chunkz on a video call with a shirtless man, discussing their goal to travel as far north as possible in England on a budget, with the shirtless man offering his car as a transport option. Throughout, the train's modern blue and grey interior, ambient lighting, and the subtle hum of the train provide a consistent backdrop to the jovial and competitive interactions.

raw_segments: The segment opens with an upbeat, slightly dramatic background track as the scene transitions from a bustling train station platform to the interior of a moving train. The Host, identifiable by his black puffer jacket with a fur-lined hood and black beanie, leads his excited group onto the train, exclaiming, 'TIME FOR US TO GET ONTO THIS TRAIN!' Once inside, the friends, including Chunkz (in a white cap and black jacket), AJ (in a dark hoodie and Jumpman cap), Sharky (in a black jacket and cap), and Shroom (in a dark jacket and beanie), settle into the blue and white seats. Laughter and animated conversation fill the air, punctuated by someone asking, 'Any more seats?' and later exclaiming 'What?' The group reacts with surprise and amusement to the train’s destination display, which shows 'Stoke-On-Trent' and 'Stockport' in red LED text. The playful banter escalates as they realize another group is on the same train, leading to a competitive exchange. Someone asks, 'What are you guys doing here?' and another replies, 'We're going to the furthest place possible.' The destinations are debated, with one person asking, 'Where are you going?' and receiving the reply 'Stoke,' followed by a declaration, 'We're going further than that.' Confusion arises as someone mentions Manchester, leading to a back-and-forth about who is going where, with one person stating, 'He's going to Stoke, I'm going Manchester.' Chunkz holds up his phone to display 'CHUNKZ IS THERE' as part of a playful challenge, while someone else remarks, 'This is jarred, man. You gotta do something different, man.' The group's excitement peaks with the realization of the other group's proximity, prompting someone to ask, 'What are the chances that we're on the same train as us?' and exclaim, 'What the hell, man!' A narrator then introduces the 'very immature Niko,' seen holding a phone and laughing, as he makes the first move in a 'childish battle.' Chunkz and AJ are described as retaliating by sending fans to annoy the other group, while sounds of playful urgency and laughter underscore the escalating rivalry.

The video captures a lively and jovial atmosphere on a modern train, complete with blue ambient lighting. The scene opens with Chunkz, wearing a white cap and black jacket, holding up his phone to display 'CHUNKZ IS THERE', prompting laughter and excited chatter from AJ, in a dark hoodie, and Sharky, in a black jacket, seated beside him. Shroom, in a beanie, also smiles, engaged in the banter, as male voices exclaim "Go, go, go, go, go!". A young boy in a vibrant purple and pink shirt approaches the group, his voice clearly heard stating, "I'm watching all of you guys right now." A male voice asks him, "Do you know Chunkz?", to which the boy confidently replies, "Yeah, of course I do," with another voice adding, "He's right there." The group, with on-screen text like 'GO' and 'I'M WATCHING ALL OF YOU GUYS RIGHT NOW', encourages the boy to take pictures. A male voice instructs him, "Just go there, take a picture, and then just keep asking him for pictures," even suggesting he pretend not to like the photo to take more. Laughter erupts as Niko, in a black jacket, initiates a playful 'chess battle' with the boy, described as 'very immature' on-screen. The boy's voice is heard asking, "Keep asking?" and later humorously stating, "I didn't get a picture." Despite this, the group praises him, with a male voice exclaiming, "He's a kid, man, take a picture!" and later, "Honestly, you're a legend. Smashed it. Well done. Smashed it," aligning with the on-screen 'HONESTLY YOU DONE A LEGEND'. Another male voice, likely Niko, suggests, "We're all one team, so we'll get one big selfie, all of us as a team," and encourages the boy to "make sure you get a picture with AJ there as well." The boy thanks them, saying, "Thank you guys for looking," as the group echoes "Legend, legend." The camera then shifts to show a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walking down the train aisle, prompting Chunkz to wave and exclaim "OH" as she passes, while the group continues their jovial interaction, with a male voice (likely Niko) mentioning, "I know that Niko's told you guys to take pictures of nonstop."

Inside a moving train, natural light streams through the windows, illuminating blue and grey seats as a group of young men and two children engage in playful, teasing banter. Chunkz, wearing a white cap and black jacket, initiates the interaction by holding up his phone to display 'CHUNKZ IS THERE,' then turns to wave at a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walking down the aisle. A young boy in a vibrant purple and pink shirt stands in the aisle, holding money, as Chunkz playfully asks him, 'Has he paid you?', to which the boy replies 'No.' Chunkz then promises, 'I'm gonna pay you,' while another man (likely AJ in a black Jumpman cap and dark hoodie) interjects about their 'budget.' The group, including Sharky in a black jacket and cap, react with laughter and mock seriousness. Chunkz instructs the boy, 'This is for you two, yeah? Just stay with them for 15 minutes and keep talking,' playfully threatening 'WE’LL THROW YOU OFF THE TRAIN' (as on-screen text) if he doesn’t comply. The boy expresses doubt, 'He's not gonna give me money,' leading to playful sounds of struggle. AJ adds, 'That's all our money. Don't want the money. This is expensive.' Chunkz reiterates, 'Sit down next to them for 15 minutes,' as the group continues with playful 'Go, go, go' sounds. The girl in the red jersey asks, 'How are you guys?' and Chunkz responds, 'Oh no, we're the same thing.' The boy then asks about 'Money?' and AJ exclaims, 'Oh my god, he paid me!' The boy, still holding money, asks 'Can I press it?' and then '50p?' after AJ states, 'We don't have any money, we got 50p.' Sharky then asks, 'Have you got change?' Chunkz delivers a mock 'quick life lesson' about how 'Blood runs thicker than money,' prompting AJ to question if the boy will 'betray us just 'cause he paid you off?' The girl in red then asks, 'How far are you going?' with AJ repeating the question, maintaining the lighthearted and mischievous atmosphere.

Inside a brightly lit train carriage with modern blue and grey seats, a jovial atmosphere unfolds as AJ, in a black Jumpman cap and Carhartt WIP long-sleeve, playfully teases, "You're not getting to Manchester... 'cause we'll throw you off the train," before initiating a negotiation with a young boy in a vibrant purple and pink shirt. AJ, in a clear, teasing voice, asks the boy to "give us that fiver" as a 'truce,' which the boy hands over with a smile amidst group laughter. AJ then jokingly accuses the boy of stealing, prompting someone, likely Sharky or Shroom, to exclaim, "He stole money off the kid!" AJ explains it's a 'test for loyalty' and proposes a deal to "split the profits" from a future game. The boy, holding the money and smiling, playfully responds in a distinct, higher-pitched voice, "you've gotta up your price sorry," and later, "you need to give us another five so it makes it fair," showcasing his negotiating skills. Chunkz, in a white cap and black jacket, joins the laughter, holding up his phone, and then exclaims a clear "OH!" as a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walks down the aisle, drawing his attention. On-screen text like 'YOU GIVE US THAT FIVER?', 'HE STOLE MONEY OFF THE KID', and 'SPLIT THE PROFITS' highlights the playful dialogue throughout the casual, urban setting.

Inside a brightly lit, moving train, the lively atmosphere is filled with chatter and the rhythmic sounds of the train. AJ, in a black Jumpman cap and Carhartt WIP shirt, initiates a playful 'chess battle' with a young boy, Niko, in a vibrant purple and pink shirt, over a '5 pound.' AJ is heard negotiating to 'split the profits now,' reminding them 'remember it was our idea for you guys to go over there.' He then playfully accuses Niko, stating, 'Niko's just taking a 5 pound off you.' Sharky, seated next to AJ, reacts with amusement, possibly exclaiming, 'You're joking.' AJ presses Niko, asking, 'Did you just give Niko some of your money?' to which Niko, holding the money, responds, 'Yeah. Because he asked for it,' eliciting laughter from the group, including Shroom who smiles. Meanwhile, Chunkz, wearing a white cap, excitedly reacts with an 'OH' and waves at a girl in a red 'HANNOVER' jersey walking down the aisle. AJ then playfully instructs Niko to 'Run up and down and say Niko stole money off us,' which Niko promptly repeats, followed by Sharky also echoing the phrase. AJ continues, advising Niko to 'walk straight past him and say, "We don't want to talk to you anymore. We've got our money,"' a line Niko also repeats. The mock confrontation culminates with AJ declaring, 'Well done! Everyone round of applause for Niko for stealing money off kids!' and 'Absolutely incredible Niko, stealing money off kids,' as humorous on-screen text like 'AMAZING WORK' and 'ABSOLUTE LEGEND' appears. Sharky adds a sarcastic 'Disgusting behavior' before AJ asks, 'Alright, is the camera off?' The group's dynamic is characterized by exaggerated gestures and expressions, all within the train's blue and grey interior.

Inside a brightly lit, modern moving train, Chunkz, wearing a white cap under a black hooded jacket, is animatedly engaged in a video call, holding his phone up. He speaks with an energetic voice, explaining that they are heading to Manchester and their ambitious goal to travel as far up into England as possible. Beside him, AJ, in a black Jumpman cap and a yellow-and-black patterned sweater, listens intently, occasionally reacting with amusement and interjecting a brief "Right." Chunkz addresses the person on the call, a shirtless man with short hair visible on the phone screen, asking if he can hear him. The shirtless man, speaking with a calm voice, confirms, "Yeah, I can hear you." Chunkz then elaborates on their budget constraints, playfully asking for money and various transport options, from a "carriage" to a "spaceship." The shirtless man calmly offers, "Would you want me to take you somewhere?" and later suggests, "obviously we've got my car." The atmosphere is casual and playful, with Chunkz laughing and gesturing as he navigates their travel logistics, all while the subtle hum of the train provides a continuous background sound.

event_blocks.summary: On a bustling Manchester street, marked by modern glass and classic red-brick buildings, Chunkz, AJ, Sharky, and Shroom walked amidst urban traffic sounds and chatter, initially discussing filming and a 'building' shot. Chunkz suddenly exclaimed, "Why do I see that demon again? Oh my god!" upon spotting a black SUV. The group reacted with amusement; Sharky laughed and AJ pointed as the driver, a man in a black jacket, rolled down his window and greeted them with a smile and a humorous, "Nah, it's my friend." Expressing disbelief and amusement, the group exclaimed, "Wow!" and playfully accused him of a convenient encounter. The driver, asserting, "I know everyone that enters this city," offered them a lift. Chunkz eagerly asked, "Can we get a lift?" and the driver agreed, stating, "I'll check in with you now." Amidst laughter, Chunkz directed everyone, "Everyone stand by the car here," towards the modern black SUV under an overcast sky, preparing to accept the ride.

The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo then suggests 'Running, we haven't done that all day,' noting he's 'tired' and that they are 'minutes away from Gretna Green,' expecting to arrive 'six minutes early.' That's the real question,' before announcing, 'We just seen a bus that is going north.' The group then exits the car and rushes toward a yellow bus at a bus stop, with the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo leading and urgently shouting to the Bus Driver, 'You right, my friend, can you just stop for a second? Please! Stop up! Please!' Another voice notes, 'He's got to go left.' As the Bus Driver initially refuses, the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo pleads, 'I'm trying to go on the bus! No! I'm trying to go to Psych! You want to help me go to Psych?' He then apologizes, 'Sorry. I'm not that fit.' He asks, 'How much is it, brother?' and after hearing 'Two pound each,' he hands a £5 note to the Bus Driver, stating, '£5. Go and use,' which the driver accepts, allowing them to board. Inside the bus, with the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo holding a 'PRICE DROP' flyer, he exclaims, 'Got tickets! Let's go! We're going north!' as another voice confirms, 'We're about to arrive in...' as they prepare to continue their journey north toward Dayna Green, Scotland.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins inside a dimly lit car at night, where the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo, Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood, and Man in Black Beanie with Water Bottle are heard cheering and shouting "Scotland!", "Into Gretna Green!", and "Welcome to Scotland!", with the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo proclaiming "We made it, guys!" and "Good work, brothers!" amidst celebratory music. The scene shifts to a coach interior where the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo and Man in White Cap (André Onana) disembark at night. The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo points to a 'Cronkeyshaw Healey Community Notice Board', remarking "This looks like the ghetto. Where the hell are we?" and stating, "We have finally reached Syke." The Man in White Cap identifies "Cronkeyshaw" and reads the notice board, adding, "The bus just left us in the middle of nowhere." Another voice confirms the location as Syke and urges them to "Go, let's go up north as much as we can." An overlaid narrator announces, "With only 10 minutes left on the clock, both teams are making the final race for victory." The group then proceeds through a dark, poorly lit street before running along a narrow, dimly lit path, chanting "We are running north!" repeatedly, accompanied by sounds of running and heavy breathing. The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo warns, "That looks dangerous," as the camera follows their urgent, potentially perilous nighttime journey, underscored by tense background music and wind sounds. Later, a nighttime drive on a highway is shown with a blue sign for 'Gretna services'. Inside the car, the Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood drives, while Chunkz is beside him. The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo emphasizes their goal to be "at the most northern point of this station." Upon exiting at Gretna Green, Scotland, excited voices, including Chunkz and the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo, repeatedly ask "Where's north?" The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo directs them, thanking "Danny." Outside, the group, including the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo and the Man in White Cap (André Onana), exits a white van. With a digital timer counting down from 3 minutes, the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo urges them to "Run!" and the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo notes they have "three minutes." Chunkz expresses reluctance, saying "I'm not going anywhere," but the group runs through a dark, wooded area, crossing a narrow wooden bridge, with sounds of running and excited shouts of "Through the woods!" Chunkz exclaims "Uh-oh!" and "Oh my god!" while the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo mentions "no booby traps." The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo urges them to "Keep going!" as the timer shows "7:59." Heavy breathing and footsteps accompany their run. The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo reiterates, "What we're doing to go north, man." As the timer hits zero, the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo exclaims, "Oh, god!" and announces, "Guys, we got to some random field in Gretna Green." They celebrate in the field, with one man holding a phone showing a map. André Onana states, "In the middle of nowhere. Surely this has got to be enough," a sentiment echoed by the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo. The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo notes the time, "It's 8 PM," and André Onana exclaims "Oh my god!" upon seeing their location, marking their arrival in Gretna Green, Scotland, at the northernmost point of their journey. The scene then shifts to a dark, grassy outdoor setting at night where the Man in White Cap (André Onana), Man in Black Cap with NY Logo, and Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo are gathered, illuminated by phone glow. On-screen text reads 'I'M SURE I'M NOT TAKING BACK WE' and 'YOU MAKE THE CALL'. The Man in White Cap asks, "You make the call?" as a female GPS voice announces "United Kingdom." The group reacts with surprise and excitement, checking their phones. Voices exclaim, "Where are they?" and "Send the location." A male voice identifies, "That's England," leading to shocked shouts of "That's England! They're in England, akh!" The Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo questions, "What do you mean England? They're in Rochdale." The Man in White Cap clarifies, "They're two hours away from us," and declares, "Wallahi we're in Scotland!" The group erupts in cheers, shouts, and laughter, jumping and hugging, with on-screen text confirming 'THAT MEANS WE WON' and 'YEAH'. They run across the field, laughing and shouting. The scene cuts to the interior of a car, where the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo and the Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood are seated, laughing and celebrating. The video ends with the group back outside, still celebrating, as the Man in Black Cap with NY Logo exclaims, "I just want to say to that Somali driver earlier, hold that! We did it without you!" Finally, in a dark, wooded outdoor setting at night, the Man in White Cap is engaged in a FaceTime video call with the Shirtless Man on Phone. The Man in White Cap playfully teases, "We didn't need you in the end. We didn't need you." The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo reveals, "So we just got the uh the the news. Look what H. And tell him why he was the reason." The Shirtless Man on Phone suggests, "Tell him you should have gave us more money." The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo explains their location: "We're we're in just above Rochdale, an area called Syke, near near Burnley." The Shirtless Man on Phone correctly guesses "Scotland" when asked where the other team is. The Man in White Cap reacts with exaggerated humor, exclaiming, "Get the out of here. Get the out of this area!" The Shirtless Man on Phone offers, "But you had a laugh though, that's all that matters." The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo humorously suggests, "See that fiver I gave you? Yeah, roll it up, stick it up your ass," prompting a quick comeback from the Man in White Cap: "Guess what? That fiver already got spent." The Shirtless Man on Phone shifts to appreciation, stating, "No, actually, he's helped us a lot today." The Man in White Cap echoes this sentiment, "So we just want to say thank you very much as well. Even though we lost, a big thank you." The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo interjects with a playful insult, "Do you know what? Do you know what, yeah? both, yeah, I hope you never go home," before the Man in White Cap attempts to conclude, "No, no, no, no, bro. We just want to say like we appreciate all the hard work and." Both men laugh and smile throughout the exchange, indicating a friendly and humorous interaction despite the dark, secluded background with minimal lighting from phone screens and a flashlight casting sharp shadows.

event_blocks.summary: At night, the Man in White Cap, wearing a 'NOCTA' cap and black puffer jacket, held a smartphone during a video call with a shirtless man, animatedly stating, "even though it was useless and we got nowhere... But we're just trying to say thank you, innit?" The Man in Black Cap with NY Logo and the Man in Black Bucket Hat with Jordan Logo stood nearby, smiling and laughing. The shirtless man on the call responded, "Honestly, I hope you never see your mum again," eliciting a loud gasp and laughter. A digital map of the UK then displayed a route from a 'STARTING POINT' to Rochdale and Gretna Green, featuring distances and icons of group members' faces. A narrator explained over dramatic music, "So Chunkz and AJ were our sore losers today. And let's have a look at the map to see how severe their defeat was," before transitioning to the victors. The scene cut to three men, including the Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood and the Man in Black Beanie with Water Bottle, standing in a dark, cold outdoor setting with visible breath, dressed in warm puffer jackets and beanies. One proposed, "If you guys want us to do this again, but we travel in planes and we go across the globe," with another adding, "Internationally. Yeah, worldwide edition." The Man in Black Beanie with Fur-Lined Hood suggested a social media challenge: "I'm saying 250,000 likes," clarified as "250 likes, you see us do this edition worldwide." After brief transitions with voices saying, "There we go," and "And with that being said, Sharky," a command, "Please play the trailer," was given. The scene shifted to a stylized dining room with red neon lights, where four men sat around a table with plates and glasses; one held a card with a green checkmark. An energetic male voice narrated over upbeat music, "Today on Beta Squad, we will all be competing against each other in a set of challenges to try and win this £10,000 pot of cash." This was followed by a man in a blue jacket holding a bucket overflowing with cash, and a man in a pink shirt raising his arms in celebration. Amidst these visuals, a crude joke was heard, "It's when the man ejaculates," followed by laughter, and a remark, "Niko's crazy." The final shot showed an elderly woman in a purple cardigan and glasses, seated, reacting with surprise and amusement, her hand covering her mouth, as excited shouts of "Liar, liar, liar!" were heard.
